doc.id: Potkay et al. - 2022 - Turgor-limited predictions of tree growth, height and metabolic scaling over tree lifespans 1.pdf
doc.title: Turgor-limited predictions of tree growth, height, and metabolic scaling over tree lifespans (Supporting Information)

model_card.name: THORP-G dynamic simulation coupling (TDGM + THORP)
task: Dynamic coupling of turgor-driven growth model (TDGM) with THORP to simulate growth and NSC pools at subdaily time steps

assumptions.text: THORP-G replaces THORP’s single NSC pool with two NSC pools: immobile sugars/starches (CS,I) and mobile sugars (CS,M).

evidence.snippet: CS ≤ 0. With the addition of the TDGM, THORP-G contains two NSC pools. Two NSC pool  503

assumptions.text: Mortality (carbon starvation) in THORP-G is determined when storage is insufficient for osmoregulation/active processes, stated as CS,I < 0 (equivalently CS < CS,M).

evidence.snippet: osmoregulation or active processes (CS,I < 0; alternatively stated as CS < CS,M).   519

assumptions.text: Realized allocation fractions ui are smoothed versions of optimal daily allocation fractions vi using an exponential-memory filter with coefficient Υ; Υ is set to 3.8×10^-6 s^-1 (≈ two-week memory).

assumptions.text: Growth in THORP-G is halted when air temperature ≤ 7°C (contrasting THORP’s ≤ 0°C threshold).

evidence.snippet: temperature was less than or equal to 7°C, based on Cabon et al.’s (2020) study of cambial growth  543

assumptions.text: NSC limitation term gNSC is represented as a Michaelis–Menten function of immobile NSC CS,I with size-dependent half-saturation coefficient KMM determined from a fixed sucrose-equivalent concentration cMM and estimated tree volume V.

parameters.name: c_{MM}
parameters.estimation: Parameterized using analogous variables from prior phloem loading PTMs (Table 2 referenced).

steps: 1) Compute xylem hydraulics (ψRc, ψS) and carbon assimilation An using THORP framework.
2) Compute optimal allocation fractions vi using THORP framework.
3) Update realized allocation fractions ui from vi using Eq. S.3.8 (smoothing filter).
4) Compute maximum potential growth G′ (and simultaneously solve cp) from ψRc, ψS, and uS via Eq. S.2.16 (TDGM).
5) Compute mobile NSC CS,M from cp using Eq. S.3.2.
6) Compute immobile NSC CS,I from CS,M using Eq. S.3.1.
7) Compute temperature limitation gT (per Schiestl-Aalto et al. 2015 / Potkay et al. 2021) and NSC limitation gNSC from CS,I using Eq. S.3.5.
8) Compute realized growth G via Eq. S.3.4.
9) Update structural biomass pools and total NSC CS per THORP carbon dynamics (including growth and respiration).

evidence.snippet: Lastly, we summarize the order of computational operations within THORP-G.  488

limitations.text: The explicit formulation for gT is not provided in this Supporting Information section; it is referenced to Eq. S.9.2b in Potkay et al. (2021) and Schiestl-Aalto et al. (2015).

limitations.text: Equations S.3.2 and S.3.6 are typeset across multiple blocks in the PDF; their exact algebraic grouping (e.g., whether V is in a denominator or multiplicative factor) is ambiguous from text extraction alone.

implementation.model_scope: Couples TDGM (turgor-limited growth) to THORP hydraulics/optimal allocation to create THORP-G with two NSC pools, temperature & NSC growth limitations, and smoothed allocation fractions.
exogenous_inputs: atmospheric forcing (radiation, VPD, etc.; via THORP)

constraints: Σ u_i = 1 (allocation fractions remain normalized when smoothing filter applied)

evidence.snippet: definition of an allocation fraction (i.e. the system must satisfy Σui = Σvi = 1, and thus also Σ∂ui/∂t  587

numerics.time_step: Subdaily time-steps (inherited from THORP; not specified here).
numerics.integration_method: Finite-difference update of allocation filter using Eq. S.3.8 (first-order linear ODE / exponential smoothing).
numerics.stability_notes: Allocation smoothing avoids high-frequency noise in daily optimal allocation fractions.

evidence.snippet: Instead, we solve Eq. S.3.7 through a finite-difference approach, requiring no storage of vi expect  583
frequency noise. The realized allocation fractions are treated as a smoothed versions of optimal  567

modules.name: thorp_hydraulics_photosynthesis
modules.responsibility: Compute ψRc, ψS and An at the current subdaily step (existing THORP).

modules.name: thorp_allocation_optimizer
modules.responsibility: Compute optimal daily allocation fractions vi (existing THORP).

modules.name: allocation_filter
modules.responsibility: Update realized fractions ui using Eq. S.3.8.

modules.name: tdgm_max_growth
modules.responsibility: Compute G′ and cp via Eq. S.2.16 using ψRc, ψS, and uS.

modules.name: nsc_pools
modules.responsibility: Compute CS,M (Eq. S.3.2) and CS,I (Eq. S.3.1); evaluate mortality criterion CS,I<0.

modules.name: growth_limiters
modules.responsibility: Compute gT and gNSC (Eq. S.3.5-6) and realized growth G (Eq. S.3.4).

modules.name: carbon_biomass_updates
modules.responsibility: Update structural pools and total NSC via THORP framework using An, G, and respiration.

open_questions: What is the exact algebraic form of Eq. S.3.2 and Eq. S.3.6 (is V in the denominator or multiplied), given the split typesetting?
What is the full formula for gT (temperature limitation) referenced to Potkay et al. (2021) Eq. S.9.2b?
What subdaily time step and numerical integration scheme does THORP-G use for state updates (An, hydraulics, carbon pools), beyond the allocation filter?

notes: Chunk C005 extracts the dynamic simulation coupling (Section S.3) describing THORP-Growth (THORP-G), including two-NSC-pool structure, growth modifiers, allocation smoothing filter, and computational step order.
Equations S.3.2 and S.3.6 are visually split in the PDF and may be ambiguous in algebraic grouping; candidates recorded.
